headline: HPE smashes Q2: revenue $10.68B (+40%), networking +148%; FY26 EPS guide nearly doubled
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise reported fiscal Q2 revenue of $10.68B vs $9.79B est, EPS $0.79 vs $0.54 est. Networking segment (Juniper) +148% YoY, Cloud & AI +23%. Management raised FY26 adjusted EPS guidance to $3.35-$3.45 (prior $2.30-$2.50) and FY26 revenue growth to 29-33%; introduced an FY27 growth framework. Shares +27-30% in after-hours / pre-market, volume ~287% of 3-month average. Read-across positive for AI server/networking demand into Broadcom (Wed AMC), AVGO already +6% pre-market.

headline: STMicroelectronics nearly doubles datacenter revenue ambition to ~$1B for 2026
summary: STM issued an intra-quarter upgrade lifting 2026 datacenter revenue guidance to about $1 billion (prior 'nicely above $500M') and said 2027 revenues could double from there. Cornerstone is the February 2026 multi-year, multi-billion-dollar AWS commercial agreement spanning silicon photonics, mixed-signal, advanced MCUs and power ICs for hyperscale. Shares +8.2% in Paris. Broadens the AI-infrastructure beneficiary list well beyond GPU/HBM names.

headline: SK Hynix to double memory wafer capacity in 5 years; AI shortage seen lasting to 2030; files US ADR
summary: SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won said at Computex on June 2 that SK Hynix will double memory wafer capacity within five years, 2026 capex will exceed the KRW 30.2T (~$20B) spent in 2025, and the AI-driven memory shortage extends through at least 2030. SK Hynix has confidentially filed for a US ADR listing targeting a June-July window, with underwriters modeling a $10-14B raise. Reinforces the structural HBM/AI memory upcycle and adds a US-listed memory pure-play for institutional investors.

headline: Morgan Stanley raises Broadcom target to $485, models $120B AI revenue by 2027 ahead of Wed earnings
summary: Morgan Stanley's Joseph Moore maintained Overweight on AVGO and lifted PT to $485 from $470 ahead of fiscal Q2 results after the close Wednesday June 3. Models AI revenue stepping from $10.8B in Q2 to $16.9B in Q3 and reaching $120B by 2027, citing room for upward revisions as ASIC programs ramp and AI networking exceeds expectations. AVGO +6.1% pre-market. Street consensus Q2 EPS $2.32 on $22.1B revenue (vs $1.58 / $15B prior-year).

headline: Asia chip names cool after run; Kospi -0.15% to 8,801 as Samsung/SK Hynix consolidate Monday's record
summary: Asia closed mixed on June 2 with the Kospi slipping 0.15% to 8,801.49 after Monday's record on Samsung +10% and the SK Hynix capacity announcement. Nikkei 225 -0.3% at 66,734; Topix -0.42%; ASX flat; Hang Seng +2.41%; CSI 300 +1.45%. Read: memory/HBM rally pausing but not breaking - chairman's 'shortage to 2030' framing keeps multi-year capex narrative intact.

headline: Trump admin moves to close AI chip export loophole for Chinese-parent entities
summary: New BIS guidance clarifies that a license is now required to sell advanced AI chips to any firm with a Chinese parent company, tightening the January 2026 framework. Targets re-export workarounds via subsidiaries in third countries. Incremental headwind for NVDA/AMD/AVGO China-rev exposure; broadly anticipated and unlikely to alter US/EU/allied demand picture but worth watching ahead of Broadcom's Wed call.

headline: Futures soft into the open; Iran/oil keeps risk premium as S&P futures dip 0.14%
summary: S&P 500 futures -0.14%, Dow -0.09%, Nasdaq -0.25% Tuesday morning after Monday's record close. Continued US-Iran exchanges keep oil bid (WTI ~$90, Brent ~$94) and trim risk sentiment. HPE blow-out is the main pre-market positive; offsets are macro/geopolitical rather than fundamental.

headline: Barclays sets street-high $665 AMD target; TD Cowen and Mizuho also weigh in
summary: Barclays set a $665 12-month price target on AMD on June 1 (the highest on the street), joined by fresh notes from TD Cowen and Mizuho. AMD consensus PT sits at $425 across 33 analysts. Bull thesis hinges on MI400/MI500 ramp and 2026 server-CPU share gains; data-center sell-out already cited by multiple desks. Read-through supports the broader AI compute capex narrative into Broadcom and Marvell prints this week.
